reasoning: While programmatic metrics indicate high text retention, the LLM fidelity report identifies two major structural and visual defects: a severely disrupted reading order where the header appears at the end, and a fragmented list of members split into a floating sidebar. Given the document's short length (169 words) and the high cost of approving structurally unsound government records, human review is required to verify accessibility and logical flow.

concerns.analysis: The reading order significantly diverges from the intended flow of the document. The primary NC DIT logo, which serves as a header in the top-left corner of the source document, has been moved to the very end of the JSON (reading_order 20). Placing header elements at the conclusion of a document is a major structural defect for document flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC DIT logo at top-left of the page.
concerns.output_quote: The image element for the logo is placed after the final paragraph with reading_order 20.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF, the 'Members Present via Teleconference' is a single continuous list of names. In the HTML rendering, this list is visually fragmented. The first five names are listed under the appropriate heading in the main content area, but the remaining seven names (Ledford through Wright) are separated from the rest of the list and positioned in a floating block on the left side of the page, beneath the 'Contents' navigation panel. This significantly disrupts the visual flow and logical
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list under 'Members Present via Teleconference' includes: Bone, Boyette, Foster, Greene, Hartman, Ledford, Martinette, Moore, O’Sullivan, Reitz, Shipp, Wright.
concerns.output_quote: The list is split. Five names (Bone through Hartman) are in the main body, while the rest (Ledford through Wright) appear as a separate bulleted list on the left side under the sidebar.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a numbered agenda (Items I through IV). While the participant lists were correctly identified as lists, this primary numbered list was converted into individual paragraphs. This loses the structural and semantic relationship between the agenda items defined in the original document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Agenda items prefixed with Roman Numerals I. through IV.
concerns.output_quote: Items like 'I. Call to Order...' and 'II. Roll Call...' are rendered as standalone 'type': 'paragraph' elements.

action_items: Relocate the NC DIT logo/header to the start of the document's reading order
Consolidate the 'Members Present via Teleconference' list into a single continuous block
Re-apply semantic list tags to the numbered agenda items
